official.name: Katie Boyd Britt
official.office_address: B40B Dirksen Senate Office Building, Washington, DC 20510

summary.narrative: Katie Britt, first-term Republican Senator from Alabama (2023–2029), entered office with campaign commitments centered on agricultural protection, pro-life policy, and China-focused economic security. As a majority party member, she has institutional leverage on legislative priorities but limited record of high-impact bill passage as of early 2025. Evidence shows early action on agricultural supply-chain security (Not One Inch or Acre Act, co-sponsored with Tom Cotton), committee participation, and constituent services positioning. However, most sponsored legislation remains in committee without passage, and measurable outcomes on flagship promises are limited.
summary.grade_rationale: Britt demonstrates policy initiation and committee engagement typical of a junior majority senator, but few promises have advanced to passage or measurable outcome. The pro-life pledge aligns with Republican caucus priorities but has not translated into independent legislative leadership. Agricultural security work (China farmland restrictions) shows concrete co-sponsorship and alignment with party priorities, earning partial credit. Constituent services and committee positioning are adequate but do not offset weak legislative delivery on stated campaign priorities.

promises.normalized: Ban Chinese nationals/entities from purchasing US farmland
Uphold sanctity of life / pro-life policies
Champion and advocate for Alabama farmers
Co-sponsor and advance agricultural security bills
Expand retirement plan access for charities/nonprofits

data_gaps: Specific outcomes and passage dates for Not One More Inch or Acre Act and related co-sponsored legislation
Detailed voting record on pro-life measures or related amendments in 118th/119th Congress
Quantified constituent services metrics (casework resolved, town halls held, etc.)
Committee assignments, subcommittee leadership, and legislative output by committee
Primary election margin and general election victory margin in 2022
Specific language from campaign platform documents or official website archives
Follow-up on retirement fairness legislation (S.4917) status as of 2025
Public statements or press releases on promise fulfillment milestones

sources.title: Sen. Katie Britt [R-AL, 2023-2028], Senator for Alabama - GovTrack.us
U.S. Senator Katie Britt Secures Historic Wins for Alabamians in First Seven Months of 119th Congress - Senator Katie Britt
